title: Week of April 6, 2026
summary: This week brought major improvements to OpsPal's reliability and automation capabilities. The platform now automatically cleans up outdated files and fixes missing dependencies during startup, preventing common session issues. Additionally, new quality checks ensure data integrity rules are properly enforced across all your revenue operations workflows.

entries.headline: Added quality checks to ensure data rules are followed
details: Every automation process now validates data before completing
Advisory processes can no longer accidentally block workflows
Faster detection when commands don't apply to current data

entries.headline: Automatic cleanup keeps your workspace running smoothly
details: Removes outdated cache files older than 7 days
Cleans up abandoned session files after 5 minutes
Warns when memory usage files become too large

entries.headline: Automatically removes broken automation scripts
details: Detects issues with any installed add-on
Prevents errors from missing or corrupted files

entries.headline: Removes outdated add-ons automatically when starting sessions
Resolved critical Salesforce workflow issues
details: Prevents invisible field problems when Dynamic Forms are active
Automatically assigns record types to avoid permission errors
Stops routing conflicts from interrupting your workflows

entries.headline: Automatically installs missing dependencies and shows clear error messages
details: Captures detailed error information during installation
Shows manual fix commands when automatic fixes fail
Runs background repairs for safe issues

entries.headline: Fixed false missing package errors during startup
